name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: ‼️‼️‼️  My amendment to the #INVEST Act, which would help families who have survived catastrophic crashes just passed the full committee (37 Aye - 27 Nays).

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: My amendment w/ @RepEspaillat, @RepCohen & @RepHankJohnson modestly increases the minimum insurance requirements & requires that it keep up w/inflation.   This isn't about trial lawyers or an attack on truckers. It’s about supporting families who have lost loved ones.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: Minimum insurance requirements for motor carriers were established in 1980 & have never been adjusted for inflation. It’s inadequate and saddles families with crippling medical care costs resulting from catastrophic crashes.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: We need to start a conversation about how our cities & our towns might look if we actually INVESTED in our transit systems. We need to reframe our work in @transportdems as a critical component of the fight for social justice & equity for everyone. Black, Brown, Rural, or Urban.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: Imagine:  ➡️ Increased access to jobs, especially for Black, Brown, and working class people  ➡️ More connectivity for city suburbs & exurbs  ➡️ Improved walkability and & potential transit access in small towns  ➡️ Reduced greenhouse gas emissions

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: My amendment seeks parity between highways and transit funding and allows us to think about what could be with increased access to affordable transportation for ALL our communities.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: I offer my #TransitEquity amendment which would quadruple transit funding without cutting highways, to highlight that we do not have to operate in a zero-sum game. We do not have to remain tied to over 60-year-old formulas to fund our infrastructure.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: Everyone can agree, our transportation systems are outdated.    For decades, we have relied on an 80-20 ratio to fund highways & transit. This split has starved our cities & all our communities from needed transit investments & opportunities for the least among us.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: Transportation policy is a social justice issue.   Across the country, our built environment has been used to segregate Black &  Brown communities. From train tracks to boulevards and highways. Our policies create or break down physical barriers to opportunity.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: This amendment forces us to look at the long-term, lifecycle costs for new construction & improves existing cost-benefit analysis requirements.   These small but important changes will help ensure we prioritize maintenance & bring our existing infrastructure up to date.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: We can build on the “fix-it-first” principles in the INVEST Act to promote maintenance, and be more fiscally responsible.   Both sides can agree that it is irresponsible to continue building new roads while simultaneously neglecting so much infrastructure already in disrepair.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: Just introduced an amendment to the INVEST Act with @repgallagher to help prioritize maintenance over building new projects and improve cost benefit analysis requirements to make sure we spend wisely.

tweet: This #coronavirus relief package, while important, falls short. More must be done in the coming weeks and the Congress must act swiftly, with urgency, to ensure all families receive the assistance they need and deserve.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: We must fund #PPP that reaches all small businesses, expands support for emergency food for families, provides financial assistance to cover rent & mortgage payments, & gives state and local governments the funding they need to protect our first responders during this pandemic.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: In the strongest possible terms, I urge my colleagues to do everything in our power to ensure that working families, and minority and immigrant owned businesses are fully included in upcoming legislation.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: The frontline workers who drive our buses, pick vegetables in the fields, draw blood at the hospital and stock the grocery store shelves need more than words of encouragement—they need financial support.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: For example, U.S.-born children of undocumented parents remain ineligible for pandemic financial relief checks; workers who contribute to our economy are excluded from assistance checks and unemployment insurance simply because they are undocumented—yet they pay taxes.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: I am deeply disappointed this package does not do more for immigrants, especially since they have been unfairly excluded from the prior legislation passed by the Congress.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: I advocated strongly for the current bill to include funds dedicated to small, minority-owned and immigrant-owned businesses, but only modest steps were taken to ensure help for these businesses.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: The Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) promised to keep thousands of businesses afloat and millions of workers employed. Yet the program ran out of funds—leaving out the majority of small businesses that keep my community vibrant.

name: Congressman Chuy García
tweet: Though we urgently need to address the effects of COVID-19, this legislation falls woefully short for the majority of small businesses and families in my district.
